title: Whats the deal with Shinji clenching his fist
display_title: Whats the deal with Shinji clenching his fist
wikitext:
[]: What's the deal with Shinji clenching his fist?             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Thu Jun 23, 2011 10:06 am IIRC there are a few scenes where Shinji is just staring at his hand and clenching it into a fist repeatedly. Perhaps most memorably when he does it on the train and gets laughed at by some younger children.

[]: Re: What's the deal with Shinji clenching his fist?             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jun 23, 2011 11:31 am Desty_Nova wrote: IIRC there are a few scenes where Shinji is just staring at his hand and clenching it into a fist repeatedly. Perhaps most memorably when he does it on the train and gets laughed at by some younger children.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Jun 23, 2011 11:32 am Why, he's remembering the feeling of Rei's boobs , of course.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 23, 2011 11:34 am esselfortium wrote: Why, he's remembering the feeling of Rei's boobs , of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jun 23, 2011 3:43 pm I feel like I already made that joke in the Episode 05 Commentary

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jun 23, 2011 4:13 pm Well, Shinji touched Rei's right breast with his left hand. There are many scenes where you see Shinji clenching his right hand and not his left. I don't think boobs, or many other possible sexual interpretations, have anything to do about it. But it might be interesting to study how Kabbalah's interpretations of the left vs right body halves fit into all of this.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 23, 2011 5:34 pm Azathoth wrote: I feel like I already made that joke in the Episode 05 Commentary

[]:             Quote Post by Cody MacArthur Fett » Thu Jun 23, 2011 5:41 pm Clenching your hands or grasping something helps you to concentrate. Sometimes there doesn't have to be a deep explanation for something.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 23, 2011 6:17 pm Cody MacArthur Fett wrote: Clenching your hands or grasping something helps you to concentrate.

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Thu Jun 23, 2011 8:19 pm the hand of eva01 Shinji: I'm the man

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jun 23, 2011 10:13 pm I just link his clenching of his hand with his experience with piloting Eva and his hesitation/decision over it.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Jun 27, 2011 5:08 pm Clenching your fist implies either determination or anger or both. Shinji typically has an apathetic attitude towards his own life, hence the clenching of his fists shows how he is slowly becoming more proactive and determined.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Mon Jun 27, 2011 9:16 pm ^ It could also mean he was feeling tense and anxious for whatever action he was going to take or whatever thought he was having too...

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jun 27, 2011 9:30 pm I actually used to stare at my hands and clench them a lot when I was in THE WAR. The callouses and the general state of disrepair they were in seemed like a physical manifestation of the great changes in my life and within myself that were occuring. It was definitely a nervous/depressive tic though. I always sort of assumed that Shinji did it for the same reason I did. I always imagined him thinking something like "the things these hands have done..."

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Jun 27, 2011 9:44 pm If you don't mind me asking, what war were you in?

[]:             Quote Post by SAWgunner » Mon Jun 27, 2011 10:00 pm The Iraq one. Don't worry, I have no problem talking about it. I didn't see all that much action anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Gecko state » Sun Aug 21, 2011 12:55 am Get the things you want in life with your own two hands.

[]:             Quote Post by MillionsKnives » Mon Aug 22, 2011 10:29 am In my view it's when Shinji's using (or trying to use) his own initiative, rather then doing what others want. The 'twich' is when hes thinking about or trying to do this, and then the clench is when hes succeeded and hes going all out. It's about him gaining confidence and getting what he wants. The scene where the kids laugh at him always struck me as deep and sad one. Shinji's just starting to feel confident, partly due to him just becoming the top sync ratio holder. Shinji clenching his fist on the bus when theres no situation for it shows that he's feeling confident in every day life. Thinking 'I can do this', but then hes laughed at for it, and it's kids laughing at him no less. Very degrading. What's worse is later on in that episode, after a second clench and show of confidence, he's f'd up by an Angel because of it. It's poor old Shinji constantly gaining confidence, being assertive, and then having the rug swept from under his feet because of it. Fueling his 'do what others tell you or do nothing' attitude to life.

[]:             Quote Post by violaine » Tue Aug 23, 2011 11:23 pm I've always gotten the impression that it was Shinji mimicking the feeling of using the Eva controller. You know, target, switch. I assumed it was a sign of him doing it a shitton, enough to give him some kind of motor twitch now. I have one sometimes after I memorize a song on the piano.

[]: Re: What's the deal with Shinji clenching his fist?             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Aug 24, 2011 1:15 am Desty_Nova wrote: Perhaps most memorably when he does it on the train and gets laughed at by some younger children.
